question: Why do atoms typically lose or gain electrons to form ions?
options: To achieve a stable noble gas electronic configuration
To increase their nucleon number
To decrease the number of protons in the nucleus
To become isotopes of the same element
answer: To achieve a stable noble gas electronic configuration

question: An aluminium atom has the electronic configuration 2.8.3. What is the electronic configuration of the Al³⁺ ion?
Which statement correctly defines isotopes?
options: Atoms with the same proton number but different nucleon numbers
Atoms with the same number of neutrons but different protons
Atoms with the same mass number but different chemical properties
Atoms with the same number of electrons but different charges
answer: Atoms with the same proton number but different nucleon numbers

question: What is the total charge of the nucleus of an atom with 11 protons and 12 neutrons?
Particle X has 17 protons, 18 neutrons and 18 electrons. What is Particle X?
options: A chloride ion with a 1– charge
A neutral chlorine atom
A chloride ion with a 1+ charge
An argon atom
answer: A chloride ion with a 1– charge

question: Why do isotopes of the same element exhibit identical chemical properties?
options: They have the same electronic configuration
They have the same number of neutrons
They have the same physical mass
They have the same number of nucleons
answer: They have the same electronic configuration

question: An atom has a nucleon number of 31 and a proton number of 15. How many neutrons does it contain?
What is the main difference between a potassium atom (K) and a potassium ion (K⁺)?
options: The ion has one fewer electron
The ion has one more proton
The ion has one more neutron
The ion has a different nucleus
answer: The ion has one fewer electron

question: An element has the electronic configuration 2.8.7. In which Group of the Periodic Table is it found?
options: Group 7
Group 1
Group 2
Group 8

question: Which particle is isoelectronic (has the same number of electrons) as a neutral neon atom (Proton number 10)?
options: Na⁺ (Proton number 11)
F (Proton number 9)
Mg (Proton number 12)
O⁻ (Proton number 8)

question: A particle contains 8 protons, 8 neutrons and 10 electrons. What is its charge?
What determines the identity of a chemical element?
options: The proton number
The number of neutrons
The number of electrons
The nucleon number

question: Three particles are described as follows: Particle J has 12 protons, 12 neutrons, 12 electrons. Particle K has 12 protons, 13 neutrons, 12 electrons. Particle L has 12 protons, 12 neutrons, 10 electrons. Which statement is correct?
options: J and K are isotopes of the same element
J and L are isotopes of the same element
K is an ion of element J
L has a different proton number than J
answer: J and K are isotopes of the same element

question: Element X has 3 electron shells and 2 valence electrons. What type of ion will it typically form?
options: X²⁺
X²⁻
X⁶⁺
X⁶⁻

question: Why is a noble gas atom generally unreactive?
options: It has a full outer electron shell
It has no electrons in its outer shell
It has equal numbers of protons and neutrons
It has a positive charge in the nucleus
answer: It has a full outer electron shell
